The latest update is out from DTXS Silk Road Investment Holdings Company Limited ( (HK:0620) ).

DTXS Silk Road Investment Holdings Company Limited has updated its board composition effective 1 April 2026, confirming a slate of executive and independent non-executive directors, with Lu Jianzhong serving as chairman. The structure underscores a separation between management and oversight roles, aiming to reinforce corporate governance standards.

The board has also formalized membership of its four key committees covering audit, nomination, remuneration and investment matters, each chaired and staffed by designated directors. This allocation of responsibilities clarifies accountability across governance functions and may enhance transparency and oversight for shareholders and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0620) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.42 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on DTXS Silk Road Investment Holdings Company Limited stock, see the HK:0620 Stock Forecast page.

The company is listed in Hong Kong and overseen by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors who manage its strategic and oversight functions.

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Shandong Weigao Group Medical Polymer Co ( (HK:1066) ) has provided an update.

Shandong Weigao Group Medical Polymer Company Limited has received a filing notice from the China Securities Regulatory Commission approving its plan for H share full circulation. The approval covers the conversion of 48,300,000 previously unlisted shares, representing about 1.06% of its issued share capital, into H shares that will be eligible for listing and trading on the Main Board of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

The filing is valid for 12 months from 26 March 2026, during which the company will seek listing and dealing approval from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange for the new H shares. The move is expected to enhance the liquidity of the company’s shares, broaden its investor base, and further integrate its shareholder structure with the international capital market.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1066) stock is a Buy with a HK$6.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shandong Weigao Group Medical Polymer Co stock, see the HK:1066 Stock Forecast page.

Shandong Weigao Group Medical Polymer Company Limited is a PRC-incorporated medical device manufacturer focused on medical polymer products and related consumables. The company serves hospital and healthcare markets and is listed in Hong Kong, accessing international capital through its H shares on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong.

Leoch International Technology Limited ( (HK:0842) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Leoch International Technology Limited has announced that its subsidiary, referred to as SpinCo, will not proceed with a planned spin-off and listing on a U.S. stock exchange. The decision follows consultations with advisors and reflects current market and regulatory conditions affecting the feasibility and timing of such an offering.

Instead of pursuing the spin-off, SpinCo will revise its financing strategies and look for alternative opportunities to support its growth and capital needs. The parent company thanked prospective investors and shareholders for their support, signaling that while this listing plan has been shelved, engagement with stakeholders and capital-market options remains ongoing.

data.description:

DTXS Silk Road Investment Holdings Company Limited ( (HK:0620) ) has issued an announcement.

DTXS Silk Road Investment Holdings announced the resignation of independent non-executive director Dai Zhijie, who is stepping down from the board, audit committee and remuneration committee from 1 April 2026 to focus on other business commitments. The board stated that Dai has no disagreement with the company and expressed appreciation for his contributions.

Following his departure, the company will temporarily fall short of Hong Kong listing requirements on the minimum number of independent non-executive directors and the composition of its audit and remuneration committees. The board plans to appoint a suitable replacement within three months of the effective date to restore compliance with governance rules, signalling a short-term corporate governance gap that investors will monitor.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0620) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.42 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on DTXS Silk Road Investment Holdings Company Limited stock, see the HK:0620 Stock Forecast page.

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Meitu ( (HK:1357) ) has provided an announcement.

Meitu has granted 16,456,530 share awards, equivalent to about 0.36% of its issued share capital excluding treasury shares, to employees and core service providers under its share award scheme, with vesting starting in April 2027. The awards, deliverable through a mix of new shares and treasury shares at the Board’s discretion, are intended to reward contributors to 2025 adjusted net profit growth, align interests with shareholders, conserve cash while expanding overseas via external consultants, and remain within Hong Kong listing rule limits, leaving 392,509,341 shares available for future grants.

The grant includes a Special Grant of 1,357 share awards each to most employees and service providers, with vesting over up to 24 months and no performance targets attached. By using equity incentives rather than cash to attract and retain talent, particularly in overseas markets where it hires through service providers, Meitu aims to support its international expansion and long-term development while maintaining compliance with individual and service provider caps under the share award scheme mandate.

More about Meitu

Meitu, Inc. is a Hong Kong-listed technology company focused on image processing, photo and video editing, and related digital services, operating through a group structure with subsidiaries and consolidated affiliates. The company is expanding rapidly overseas, often relying on external service providers in markets where it is not yet practical to establish local subsidiaries to support its international growth.

Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited ( (HK:2788) ) has issued an update.

Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for April 23, 2026, in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025 and vote on a proposed final dividend of HK$0.77 per share. Investors will also decide on the re-election of eight retiring directors, the authorisation of the board to set directors’ pay, the re-appointment of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu as auditor, and a general mandate empowering the board to issue additional shares, which could provide future funding flexibility and affect capital structure.

The proposed dividend signals management’s confidence in the company’s 2025 performance and offers a tangible return to shareholders ahead of the new financial year. The comprehensive slate of director re-elections and the renewal of the share issuance mandate underscore continuity in governance and strategic optionality, developments that are likely to be closely watched by investors assessing the company’s future growth plans and governance stability.

Hygieia Group Limited issued a clarification regarding its previously published annual results announcement for the year ended 31 December 2025, correcting an omission about the currency used for its proposed final dividend.   • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Greentech Technology International Limited ( (HK:0195) ) just unveiled an update.

Greentech Technology International has issued a further update on its prolonged compliance challenges, which have led to delays in publishing its 2024 interim and annual results and its 2025 interim results, as well as a continuing suspension of trading in its shares. The company has been working under resumption guidance and has already conducted an independent forensic investigation and internal control review as part of efforts to address regulatory concerns.

In the latest development, Greentech disclosed details of an anonymous complaint lodged with its auditor, prompting the appointment of OCF as an independent investigator and now the engagement of an additional independent investigator. The allegations center on allegedly questionable fund flows at a former subsidiary between 2020 and 2021 and a purported payment to a former director from proceeds of a loan repayment, deepening scrutiny of the group’s past transactions and governance and potentially impacting investor confidence and the timetable for trading resumption.

More about Greentech Technology International Limited

Greentech Technology International Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating through a group structure that has included various subsidiaries. The company is currently subject to trading suspension on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong due to delayed financial reporting and ongoing regulatory and investigative matters affecting its recent interim and annual results.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Weigang Environmental Technology Holding Group Ltd. ( (HK:1845) ) has issued an update.

Weigang Environmental Technology Holding Group Ltd. has appointed Xu Anli as joint company secretary effective 1 April 2026, highlighting her decade of experience in corporate investment, governance, compliance, and IPO matters, as well as her tenure as the company’s assistant secretary since December 2022. The board argues that her deep familiarity with the group’s operations and disclosure practices will strengthen secretarial functions and support corporate governance, capital management, and investor relations.

Because Xu does not yet meet the formal company secretary qualifications under Hong Kong Listing Rule 3.28, Weigang obtained a three-year conditional waiver from the Stock Exchange, requiring that she be assisted by qualified joint secretary Ms. Pang and complete at least 15 hours of relevant training annually. The waiver, which can be revoked in case of material Listing Rules breaches, underscores both the exchange’s flexibility and the company’s dependence on Xu’s expertise to maintain regulatory compliance while its main business remains outside Hong Kong.

Palasino Holdings Limited has announced the composition of its board of directors effective 1 April 2026, confirming Pavel Maršík as the sole executive director and chief executive officer, alongside chairman Tan Sri Dato’ David Chiu and non-executive director Cheong Thard Hoong. data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Everbright Water ( (SG:U9E) ) just unveiled an announcement.

China Everbright Water has secured two new wastewater treatment projects in Jiangsu Province, extending its footprint in the Yangtze River Delta. The company will invest about RMB90 million in Phase III of the Huai’an Huaiyin Eastern District Waste Water Treatment Project under a BOT model, adding 20,000 m3 of daily capacity and aligning the concession with existing phases through 2050.

It also won the EPC+O contract for Phase I Stage 2 of the Jiangyin High-tech Zone Waste Water Treatment Plant, further strengthening its portfolio of municipal water projects. These wins deepen Everbright Water’s cooperation with local governments, optimise its business mix in advanced industrial regions and reinforce its positioning as a leading regional water environment services provider.

More about China Everbright Water

China Everbright Water Limited is an environmental protection company specialising in water environment management across China, with shares listed in Hong Kong and Singapore. The company focuses on municipal wastewater treatment and related engineering, procurement, construction and operation services, with a growing presence in key economic regions such as the Yangtze River Delta.

An announcement from China Wah Yan Healthcare ( (HK:0648) ) is now available.

SoftMedx Healthcare Limited has completed the issuance of new scheme shares under a specific mandate, following the fulfillment of all conditions precedent on 1 April 2026. The transaction modestly increased the company’s total share capital to about 2.14 billion shares and introduced a trustee as a new public shareholder, slightly diluting the controlling stake of Multi Omniverse Group Limited from 74.9% to 74.4% while marginally broadening the public float.

The revised shareholding structure leaves the controlling shareholder’s influence effectively intact but signals a small step toward diversifying ownership through the scheme share arrangement. For minority investors, the change marginally enhances liquidity and public participation in the stock, although governance and strategic control remain firmly in the hands of the dominant shareholder group.

More about China Wah Yan Healthcare

SoftMedx Healthcare Limited, formerly known as China Wah Yan Healthcare, operates in the healthcare sector and is listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The company’s ownership is highly concentrated, with controlling shareholder Multi Omniverse Group Limited holding roughly three-quarters of its issued share capital, while the remainder is held by public investors, including a trustee structure and other minority shareholders.

JD Logistics, Inc. ( (HK:2618) ) has issued an announcement.

JD Logistics has granted 4,250,355 new share awards under its Post-IPO Share Award Scheme to employees of the group, excluding directors and senior management, representing about 0.06% of its issued share capital as of the grant date. The awards, granted at a market price of HK$14.14 per share, vest in tranches over periods ranging from 0.25 to four years, with some shorter vesting linked to batch administration, and are designed to incentivize and retain staff without attaching specific performance targets.

The new grants incorporate detailed clawback provisions that allow the company to forfeit or cancel unvested and even vested shares, and reclaim related proceeds, in cases such as dismissals without notice, criminal convictions involving integrity or honesty, or conduct damaging to the group’s interests or reputation. These grants are also subject to broader clawback rules tied to potential financial restatements at JD Logistics or its parent, signaling a strong emphasis on compliance, governance, and alignment of employee interests with long-term shareholder value.
